notes: Performances on par with the game on Windows. It ran perfectly from start to finish, in both singleplayer and multiplayer with friends playing on Windows.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Runs fine, doesn't complain when changing settings.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Some soundglitches occur but runs perfectly fine otherwise. For those that wonder, the Intel HD 620 is wholly underpowered for this game, but it is enough for 800x600 resolution :P
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Helldivers didn't start with any of the versions of proton available at the time
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: No tricks needed, just run this in the terminal in order to run the game online: symlinking "/usr/lib//libgnutls.so.30.14.11" to "$HOME/.steam/steam/SteamApps/common/Proton 3.7/dist/lib/libgnutls.so.26. 10/10 amazing performance
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Only starts with PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1, but then it works perfectly.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Runs nearly flawlessly provided you select the "Launch 32-bit in Safe Mode" option before launch, launching normally resulted in some ear obliterating audio bugs that continued happening even with the in-game audio muted. 32-bit Safe Mode doesn't seem to downgrade anything within the game, from what I could tell. Outside of this some lightning attacks from the blue enemies became rainbow disco lightning, but this didn't harm gameplay and was rare. The symlink fix for multiplayer mentioned in other reports seems to be no longer required.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: The game runs fine and without any issues. Performance and stability has significantly improved over older proton versions.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Everything seems to work just fine, including multiplayer (controller not tested)
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: No tweaks needed. Everything seems to work as expected. Running 1440p@60fps stable for 2 missions.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Runs fine if you select 32-bit mode (otherwise you will have really bad audio glitches). There are issues with some effects after a while, they will blow out and be crazy coloured. There is also some crackling still with the audio, but generally it's playable.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Doesn't start in normal or 32 bit safe mode.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Game crashes if mission is failed, or if I try to view my friends list when connecting to multiplayer.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Adding "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" to launch options (without quotes) and running in 32bit mode. Runs flawlessly now.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: The game runs well, but the sounds for the flamethrower and the gatling gun on the mechs are borked and will break your ear drums. However, the game does not have these issues and runs flawlessly if you play on 32-bit safe mode.
title: HELLDIVERS™ A New Hell Edition

notes: Even with tweaks noted by others the sound consistently crackles and pops. Other than that the game plays without any other noticeable issues.
